card.intuition: Ensuring custom web fonts load quickly and don't cause visual jitter (like text suddenly changing size or jumping) during page load.
card.analogy: A restaurant sign: if the custom neon sign (Web Font) is broken, you'd rather see a plain painted sign (Fallback Font) immediately than have a blank, empty wall for 30 seconds while the electrician arrives.
when_to_use: Reducing CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) caused by font swaps
Speeding up the time to 'First Contentful Paint'
gotchas: FOUT (Flash of Unstyled Text): the user sees a system font before the web font pops in. FOIT (Flash of Invisible Text): the user sees NOTHING until the font loads
Self-hosting fonts is often faster than using Google Fonts because it avoids an extra DNS lookup and connection to a 3rd party server

steps.label: Discover
steps.annotation: Browser parses @font-face — font not downloaded yet (lazy).

steps.label: FOIT/FOUT
steps.annotation: Font loading delay causes invisible text (FOIT) or fallback swap (FOUT).

steps.label: Preload
steps.annotation: Preloading font hints browser to fetch early — reduces FOIT.

steps.label: Subsetting
steps.annotation: Only load glyphs needed for the page language — smaller file.

steps.label: font-display
steps.annotation: font-display: swap shows fallback immediately, swaps when ready.

exercise.prompt: Identify the CSS property used to control the FOUT/FOIT behavior.
hints: It determines how the font is 'displayed'
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "display"
assert check("display")
print("PASS")
